response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer with broad full-stack background and some relevant geospatial experience through GISCollective, plus C++ exposure at National Instruments and C++ on a bachelor thesis project. However, the role requires modern C++ (or Rust) as the primary skill, and the candidate's recent decade has been dominated by TypeScript/Node.js/DLang work — no recent commercial C++ or Rust experience is evident, and no distributed-systems navigation/routing pipeline work is shown. The GIS domain familiarity and low-latency DLang work at Sociomantic partially offset the gap, making this a borderline screen worth a short technical conversation rather than a clear pass.
strengths: Geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map (GIS data, mapping)
Low-latency, high-throughput backend work at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, millions of req/s)
Senior-level breadth with Linux, distributed systems exposure, and prior C/C++ at National Instruments
concerns: No recent modern C++ or Rust commercial experience — primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
No demonstrated experience with routing tiles, map data pipelines, or navigation guidance systems
C++ work is over 10 years old (NI, 2014-2015); modern C++17/20/23 proficiency unclear

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent modern C++/Rust experience
